summary: Write integration tests against the IStorage contract
problem_statement: The storage layer is the persistence backbone of the application, but it has no automated tests verifying that its methods behave according to their contract. Bugs in storage logic (incorrect queries, missing fields, constraint violations) propagate silently to every route handler. Contract tests verify each IStorage method against expected inputs and outputs.
in_scope: Write Vitest integration tests that exercise each IStorage method defined in P2-R2-07
Test CRUD operations for tasks, stages, and sub-stages through the storage interface
Test edge cases: not-found lookups, invalid inputs, constraint violations where applicable
Use a test database instance or in-memory equivalent to run against real storage behaviour
out_of_scope: Changing the storage implementation — tests verify the existing behaviour
Database schema changes — the existing Drizzle schema remains unchanged
Testing storage methods not part of the IStorage contract
requirements: Each IStorage method has at least one test covering its success path
Edge cases (not-found, invalid input) are tested for critical methods
Tests run via npm run test and use the Vitest framework
Tests are isolated (each test starts with a known state)
acceptance_criteria: Automated tests exist for storage contract behaviour
All tests pass via npm run test
Tests cover CRUD operations for the primary domain entities (tasks, stages, sub-stages)
npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
dependencies: P2-R2-07 — IStorage contract interface must be defined
risks: Integration tests against a real database are slower and require setup/teardown — consider using a test database or in-memory adapter
Test isolation requires cleaning or resetting the database between tests
implementation_notes: If the project uses an in-memory storage implementation (e.g. MemStorage), test against that for speed. If only a database-backed implementation exists, use a dedicated test database with setup/teardown in beforeEach/afterEach. Test against the IStorage interface, not the concrete implementation, so tests validate contract compliance.
